fields.description:
# Live-store remediation: drive fsck to zero (safe, reversible, batched)

## Why
Historical corruption is resident: `rebar fsck` = 2422 SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT + 11 ORPHAN_EVENT + 1
MISSING_CREATE. Fix #1 stopped new corruption; this cleans the backlog. DESTRUCTIVE on live shared data —
safety/reversibility are first-class.

## Repair per fault class (each needs a different fix)
- SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT (2422): fault = a folded source file STILL PRESENT (listed in `source_event_uuids`
  but never deleted). Repair = retire (`.retired`, per A1) each still-present listed source — replicating what
  compaction should have done. NOT a full rebuild.
- ORPHAN_EVENT (11): a merged-in pre-snapshot event absent from `source_event_uuids` -> use A2's rebuild-on-stray
  path (rebuild from the full ordered log incl. `.retired`) so the orphan lands in true position.
- MISSING_CREATE (1): no valid CREATE reduces -> human triage.

## Safety (resolves the destructive-op finding)
- Pre-remediation backup: `git -C <tracker> tag pre-a3-remediation <oid>` (+ optional `rebar export`) BEFORE
  any write. Rollback: `git -C <tracker> reset --hard pre-a3-remediation && git push --force-with-lease`.
- Dry-run FIRST: `fsck --repair --dry-run` prints per-ticket before/after diff + orphan dispositions and writes
  NOTHING; the live run is gated on human review of that diff.
- Idempotent + resumable: per-snapshot write-tmp-then-atomic-rename + a per-ticket "repaired" marker so a re-run
  skips completed tickets. NOTE: A2's compaction skips tickets that already have a SNAPSHOT, so repair must
  delete/replace the bad SNAPSHOT explicitly.
- Batched/throttled: `fsck --repair --limit=200`, wait >=30s between batches to drain queued writes.
- Reconciler paused via the phase-gate (`refs/reconciler/gate` -> OBSERVE via `_advisory_lock.check_phase_gate`),
  verified inactive before start; failsafe auto-resume after N minutes / on repair-process exit so it is never
  left paused indefinitely.

## Orphan disposition policy
- AUTO-RECOVER (additive, safe out of order): COMMENT, LINK, UNLINK, TAG_DELTA, COMMITS.
- HUMAN TRIAGE (LWW-scalar; folded siblings hard-deleted pre-A1 -> correct order unrecoverable): STATUS, EDIT
  (title/description/priority/assignee/parent), FILE_IMPACT, VERIFY_COMMANDS, SIGNATURE-mirror. Decision recorded
  in the PR / a linked ops doc; outcomes: recover / quarantine to an `orphan-triage/` prefix / documented drop.

## Scope
`_commands/fsck.py` (`--repair` + `--dry-run` + `--limit`), `_commands/compact.py` (retire-still-present path),
`reducer/_api.py` (include-retired rebuild, shared with A2). Depends on A1 + A2 (wired). Gated on RC1 fix #1 (deployed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `fsck --repair --dry-run` prints per-ticket before/after + dispositions, writes nothing; live run gated on review.
- [ ] Pre-remediation backup tag created; rollback procedure documented AND rehearsed on a fixture.
- [ ] Repair idempotent/resumable (per-ticket marker; atomic per-snapshot write) and batched (<=200/batch).
- [ ] Reconciler paused via phase-gate for the run (verified inactive) with a failsafe auto-resume.
- [ ] Synthetic-fixture unit tests: known SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT / ORPHAN_EVENT / MISSING_CREATE (incl. `.retired`) repair correctly.
- [ ] Live store reaches 0 UNRESOLVED SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT / ORPHAN_EVENT / MISSING_CREATE (triaged+documented orphans count as resolved); before/after counts recorded.
